rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA ADVOCATES FOR NURSING HOME REFORM et al VS. SANDRA SHEWRY

rulings.Case Title: DONALD FULTON et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 16, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant is not a proper party in this case because plaintiff failed to seek leave to amend to name defendant as a Doe defendant under CCP 474. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD FULTON et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 16, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT DEERE & COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof under McGonnel v. Kaiser Gypsum Co. (2002) 98 Cal.App. 4th 1098. Although special interrogatories are desirable, they are not mandatory to shift the burden of producing evidence. Decedent's deposition testimony and the other evidence presented is sufficient to do so. Plaintiff fails to present evidence giving rise to a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' et al VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn In Support Of Mtn For Attorneys' Fees Pursuant To 42 U.S.C. Sec 1988
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 16, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANTS STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINSTRATION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS, BRIGID HANSON, RODERICK HICKMAN AND TIM VIRGA'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees Pursuant To 42 U.S.C. Sec 1988 is denied. Defendant is correct that 42 USC section 1988 provides a separate basis apart from CCP 1038 for defendant to seek attorneys' fees having prevailed in this action. However, defendant may not make seriatim motions that seek the same relief; rather defendant was obligated to put forth all of its reasons for an award of attorneys' fees when defendant made the initial request. This is the teaching of Baldwin v. Home Savings of America (1997) 59 Cal. App. 4th 1192. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARK PRINCIPE et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DBA S.F. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 16, 2008, line 14. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant has not produced undisputed facts sufficient to shift the burden of producing evidence in that defendant did not address the facts existing on the bus prior to plaintiff boarding. Plaintiff also has presented triable issue of fact that are sufficient to place the driver on notice of the need to protect a passenger and the harm occurred as a direct consequence of a failure to do so. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DANIEL YAUYEN WONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 16, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT NATIONAL GENERAL CONTRACTING INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on the Chan declaration. Plaintiff fails to present evidence of a triable issue of fact based on alter ego or common counts. Plaintiff's procedural objections are not a basis to deny the motion. The only evidence is that Wells Fargo had an account with Mr. Wong and a sole proprietorship, National Gerneral Contracting Co. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DANIEL YAUYEN WONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 16, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT NATIONAL GENERAL CONSTRUCTION, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on the Kho declaration. Plaintiff fails to present evidence of a triable issue of fact based on alter ego or common counts. Plaintiff's procedural objections are not a basis to deny the motion. The only evidence is that Wells Fargo had an account with Mr. Wong and a sole proprietorship, National Gerneral Contracting Co. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DANIEL YAUYEN WONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 16, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT WALTER CHAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The Chan declaration shifts the burden of producing evidence. Defendant fails to present evidence giving rise to a triable issue of fact on the alter ego, common counts or implied in fact contract. The record is silent that Wells Fargo was looking to Chan as an obligor on this account. Plaintiffs procedural objections are not a basis to deny the motion. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CHRIS E. CARNES VS. LAURIE MARTIN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 16, 2008, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT CHRIS CARNES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the 3rd and 4th causes of action is overruled. The cross-complaint is replete with facts that provide a basis for the claims. Corp. Code section 16405 (a) authorizes the breach of contract claim. Demurrer is sustained as to the 7th cause of action without leave to amend. The facts set forth in the cross-complaint negate the contention that cross-complainant was a dependent adult within the meaning of the Welfare & Institutions Code. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. JOHN ROE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 16, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT JEAN MAGUIRE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The decisions in Dutra v. Eagleson (2006) 146 Cal. App. 4th 216, 222 and Aaronoff v. Martinez-Senftner (2006) 136 Cal. App. 4th 910, hold that the claims as to this defendant are barred by the statute of limitations CCP 340.1(a)(2), (3), and (b)(1). =(302/PJM/PB)
